# Copilot instructions

## Project overview

This is a dependency-free, offline-first private pilot learning notebook. Open
`index.html` directly in a browser; there is no package manager, build step,
development server, or network requirement.

The application is a static single-page site:

- `index.html` defines the fixed views and loads scripts in dependency order:
  note data, vocabulary data, bundled KaTeX, then application behavior.
- `content/notes.js` and `content/vocabulary.js` are data sources exposed as
  `window.PPL_NOTES` and `window.PPL_VOCABULARY`. Keep learning content here
  rather than embedding it in HTML or application code.
- `assets/app.js` owns hash routing, rendering, search/filtering, flashcards,
  quizzes, speech synthesis, and mastery persistence in `localStorage`.
- `assets/styles.css` styles the fixed DOM and the markup generated by
  `assets/app.js`. IDs in `index.html`, render functions in `app.js`, and CSS
  selectors are tightly coupled and must be updated together for UI changes.
- KaTeX and all required fonts are bundled in `vendor/katex`; images are local
  under `images/` so the notebook remains usable offline.

## Validation

There is no automated test suite, linter, or single-test command. Run the
existing syntax and whitespace checks after JavaScript or content changes:

```sh
node --check content/notes.js
node --check content/vocabulary.js
node --check assets/app.js
git --no-pager diff --check
```

Then open `index.html` directly and exercise the affected flow. For content
changes, confirm the note, local image, display/inline TeX, vocabulary
pronunciation, flashcard, and quiz views render correctly as applicable.

## Content conventions

For normal learning-material additions, change only `content/notes.js`,
`content/vocabulary.js`, and local files in `images/`. Do not modify
`index.html`, `assets/`, or `vendor/` unless the requested behavior cannot be
represented by the existing content schema.

Append notes to `window.PPL_NOTES` with a unique stable kebab-case `id`, concise
`summary`, category, useful search tags, the actual `updated` date in
`YYYY-MM-DD` format, and structured `sections`. Supported section types are
`text`, `question`, `formula`, `image`, `comparison`, and `takeaway`; follow
their existing shapes rather than adding presentation markup to content.

Write explanations primarily in Traditional Chinese unless requested
otherwise, while preserving important English aviation terminology. Prefer
short sections that retain the learner's question, explain the reasoning, and
end with practical pilot implications. Keep technical claims accurate,
distinguish intuition from precise definitions, include units, and define
formula symbols.

Formula strings contain TeX and therefore require escaped JavaScript
backslashes. A `formula` field renders as display math; single dollar signs in
body text render inline math. Store images locally with descriptive kebab-case
names and accurate `alt` text; do not make remote resources required for
content.

Append vocabulary to `window.PPL_VOCABULARY` without duplicate `term` values.
Use standard American English IPA where practical, a concise Traditional
Chinese `translation`, contextual detail in `explanation`, useful
root/prefix/suffix or etymology in `breakdown`, and an established aviation
`topic` such as `Aeromedical`, `Weather`, `Navigation`, or
`Flight Instruments`.

## Application conventions

The browser code uses plain JavaScript inside a strict-mode IIFE, with no
modules or framework. Preserve the global content contracts and script order.
Route changes use URL hashes (`#notes`, `#vocabulary`, and `#note/<id>`), while
mastered vocabulary terms are persisted under `ppl-note-mastered-v1`.

When generating HTML in `assets/app.js`, escape content with `escapeHtml`.
Use `renderRichText` only where inline TeX is intentionally supported. Keep
event delegation based on existing `data-*` attributes for generated lists and
controls.

Do not commit raw source material after it has been accurately migrated into
the structured content unless explicitly requested.
